description: Canonical ordered list of SOUL.md sections. The validator checks that every SOUL contains the required H2 headings in order; the template and docs are generated from this file. To evolve the format, change this file in one place.

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.prompt: Why does this way of thinking exist? What does the world get from it that nothing else provides?

sections.heading: Core Mission
sections.prompt: The single sentence the practitioner would defend under pressure. What are they ultimately responsible for?

sections.heading: Primary Responsibilities
sections.prompt: The concrete work. What actually fills the days and where attention goes.

sections.heading: Guiding Principles
sections.prompt: The values and commitments that shape judgment when no rule applies.

sections.heading: Mental Models
sections.prompt: The reusable internal representations experts use to compress reality and predict outcomes.

sections.heading: First Principles
sections.prompt: The irreducible truths of the domain that everything else is reasoned up from.

sections.heading: Questions Experts Constantly Ask
sections.prompt: The recurring questions running in the background of an expert's mind.

sections.heading: Decision Frameworks
sections.prompt: Repeatable structures for making the hard, recurring calls of the role.

sections.heading: Workflow
sections.prompt: How work actually flows from trigger to completion, including the loops and checkpoints.

sections.heading: Common Tradeoffs
sections.prompt: The competing goods that can never all be maximized at once, and how experts navigate them.

sections.heading: Rules of Thumb
sections.prompt: Compressed heuristics that are usually right and cheap to apply.

sections.heading: Failure Modes
sections.prompt: How the work goes wrong in practice — the predictable, recurring failures.

sections.heading: Anti-patterns
sections.prompt: Tempting approaches that look right but reliably produce bad outcomes.

sections.heading: Vocabulary
sections.prompt: The terms of art an insider uses precisely and an outsider misuses.

sections.heading: Tools
sections.prompt: The instruments, software, and equipment of the trade, and what each is really for.

sections.heading: Collaboration
sections.prompt: Who the practitioner works with, how, and where the friction and handoffs live.

sections.heading: Ethics
sections.prompt: The duties, lines that must not be crossed, and the gray zones that demand judgment.

sections.heading: Scenarios
sections.prompt: Concrete situations worked through end to end, showing the reasoning in motion.

sections.heading: Related Occupations
sections.prompt: Neighboring minds — what they share and where they diverge.

sections.heading: References
sections.prompt: Bodies of knowledge, standards, and sources worth studying.
